title: peptide_nanobot_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS W1a output rows
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/peptide_nanobot_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS integration W1a: a peptide covalently attached to a DNA-origami / C0b nanostructure displays only the conformation it pre-organizes into, so the NANOBOT core-5 axis's display orientation is bounded by the PEPTIDE :> WEAVE sub-axis's Zimm-Bragg helix-coil cooperative pre-organization. For each device in a deterministic peptide-decorated-nanostructure panel, the PEPTIDE sub-axis's helix-coil partition (peptide_sim.py, imported verbatim — no fork; Zimm-Bragg 1959 / Lifson-Roig 1961) computes the fractional helicity theta_H, and a display-orientation-constraint score (= theta_H ∈ [0,1]) is gated against an illustrative display-orientation gate. CRITICAL RESEARCH-STAGE HONESTY: peptide-decorated DNA nanostructures are an ACTIVE RESEARCH FIELD (Pinheiro / Bertozzi-lab-style peptide-DNA conjugates; Schmidt-Mende-style peptide-decorated nanostructures) but are NOT an FDA-approved drug class. No FDA-approved peptide-decorated DNA nanobot therapeutic exists. The peptide panel and the theta_H→display-orientation-constraint mapping are illustrative modeling choices. In-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8/f2) — NOT a structural / binding / display-orientation / nanodevice-function / therapeutic / regulatory claim. No theta_H / constraint / count is derived from the n=6 lattice (g2/f_lattice_fit). Modality precedent is researcher-class (Rothemund 2006 DNA origami + peptide-DNA conjugate research), NEVER lattice-derived (g3/f1). A cross-axis bridge is NOT a new axis: the hexa-bio core-5 set (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED.

device_id.description: Identifier of the peptide-decorated DNA nanostructure from the deterministic device panel.

device_note.description: Illustrative design note describing the nanostructure-class + attached-peptide-class.

attached_peptide_sequence.description: Single-letter amino-acid sequence of the peptide covalently attached to the DNA nanostructure.

n_residues.description: Number of residues in the attached peptide.

anchor_position.description: Illustrative anchor position on the DNA nanostructure (e.g. staple 5'-amine, staple 3'-azide).

nanobot_axis_source.description: Path to the NANOBOT core axis source whose DNA-nanotech modality basis is read as structural text (actuation logic untouched — f3).

nanobot_axis_role.description: Role of the NANOBOT core-5 axis (DNA-nanotechnology actuation).

modality_basis_cited_by_axis.description: DNA-nanotech modality citations the NANOBOT axis declares for itself (Rothemund 2006 DNA origami / Seeman 1982 immobile junctions) — own precedent, never lattice-derived (g3/f1).

peptide_model.description: The PEPTIDE :> WEAVE sub-axis model string — must reference Zimm-Bragg helix-coil theory.

sigma_nucleation.description: Zimm-Bragg helix-nucleation parameter sigma (<< 1 makes the helix-coil transition cooperative).

partition_Z.description: Zimm-Bragg partition function Z = Σ σ^(#nucleations) * Π s_i — must be strictly positive.

fractional_helicity_theta_h.description: Boltzmann-weighted Zimm-Bragg fractional helicity theta_H ∈ [0,1] of the attached peptide.

independent_residue_helicity_sigma_to_1.description: sigma → 1 (no nucleation cost) independent-residue baseline helicity, used as a closed-form cross-check (theta_H ≤ this baseline always).

cooperativity_suppresses_helix.description: True iff theta_H ≤ sigma→1 baseline (a nucleation penalty can only suppress helix).

display_orientation_constraint.description: Display-orientation-constraint score for the attached peptide ∈ [0,1]: 0 = unoriented flexible tether, 1 = rigid pre-organized rod. Defined as theta_H by construction.

display_orientation_constraint_in_unit_i.description: Must be true — display_orientation_constraint is a fraction by construction.

display_orientation_gate.description: Display-orientation gate: oriented_display iff display_orientation_constraint ≥ this gate. Illustrative fixed value, NOT lattice-derived.

display_orientation_margin.description: display_orientation_constraint minus display_orientation_gate. Non-negative iff oriented_display.

oriented_display.description: True iff display_orientation_constraint ≥ display_orientation_gate — the PEPTIDE-axis Zimm-Bragg bound on the NANOBOT-axis display orientation.

in_silico_caveat.description: Restatement that every row is in-silico simulator-consistency only (g8/f2).

illustrative_only.description: Always true — the peptide panel, sigma nucleation parameter, and theta_H→display-orientation-constraint mapping are illustrative; the row is not a prediction of real peptide-decorated-nanostructure behaviour.

creates_a_new_axis.description: Always false — this cross rests on existing :> WEAVE PEPTIDE sub-axis + core NANOBOT axis; core-5 set (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED.

research_stage_modality.description: Always true — peptide-decorated DNA nanostructures are an active research field but NOT an FDA-approved drug class. There is no FDA-approved peptide-decorated DNA nanobot therapeutic. The modality precedent is researcher-class (Pinheiro / Bertozzi-lab-style peptide-DNA conjugates; Schmidt-Mende-style peptide-decorated nanostructures), never a drug-class claim.
